data.task:
Surface the founder-facing run display number in the workspace Runs tab and rename the tab. Files: packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx and a new/extended UI test.

CHANGE 1 — Rename the tab. In the TabStrip tabs array (Dashboard.tsx:74), change `label: 'Runs/Sessions'` to `label: 'Runs'`. Leave the tab id ('runs') and count wiring unchanged.

CHANGE 2 — Make the run's display number the PRIMARY label of each run row in RunsTab (Dashboard.tsx ~lines 240-254). Today the prominent line is `run.title` (line 247) and `runDisplayName(run)` only appears small/muted in the persona row (line 249). Render `runDisplayName(run)` (already imported from '../../model.ts'; it returns the founder-facing display name e.g. 'workspace run 98', falling back to run.id) as a prominent primary label at the top of the row's text column — visually at least as prominent as the title (same or larger font weight/size as the existing title line), with `run.title` kept as a secondary line beneath it. Keep the technical id available but secondary: you may keep/move the existing muted `runDisplayName(run)` occurrence in the persona row, but ensure the technical `run.id` is NOT the primary handle. Do not change the drag payload (`setOzItemDragData(e, 'run', run.id, runDisplayName(run))`), the StatusChip, the priority/ad-hoc subline, the personas chips, or the grid layout beyond what is needed to add the prominent label.

CHANGE 3 — Pin both with a test. Add a focused React test (new file packages/ui/tests/dashboard-runs-tab.test.tsx, mirror the setup/imports of the existing packages/ui/tests/dashboard-awaiting.test.tsx — same render harness, testing-library, vitest). It must render the Dashboard (or RunsTab via the dashboard) with a runs fixture where a run has `displayName: 'workspace run 98'` (and displayNumber: 98) and assert: (a) the Runs tab button label is exactly 'Runs' and the string 'Runs/Sessions' does NOT appear; (b) after activating the Runs tab, the run row shows 'workspace run 98' as its primary label. Make assertion (b) target the prominent/primary element specifically (e.g. by role/structure or by asserting the display name renders, not merely that the substring exists somewhere), so it genuinely pins the primary-label requirement and fails if the number is demoted back to a muted secondary spot.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK:
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/ui test` passes including the new test (run it and paste the exact pass/fail output and the new test's result).
- UI typecheck passes: `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/ui run typecheck` (paste exit status).
- Do NOT modify the core/adapter `runDisplayName` vocabulary or persona/Oz surfaces in this atom — that is a later atom. Scope is Dashboard.tsx + the new test file only.
- Do not regress the existing dashboard-awaiting test or other tabs.

Report: the exact diff, the test command output (pass counts), and the typecheck exit status.

data.reason: Tab renamed to 'Runs'; run row promotes displayName to a prominent level-3 heading with title secondary; new test pins both (heading-role assertion enforces primacy); ui test 166/166 pass and typecheck exits 0.

data.task:
Make the founder-facing run-reference vocabulary owner able to emit the REAL workspace name ('CoCoder run 98') instead of the literal word 'workspace', additively and back-compat. This is the taxonomy owner; later atoms thread the name through consumers. Files: packages/core/src/store/portable/display.ts and packages/core/tests/display.test.ts ONLY.

CHANGE 1 — Extend the input. In display.ts, add an optional field to `RunDisplayInput`:
  readonly workspaceName?: string | null
Keep `id` and `displayNumber` exactly as they are.

CHANGE 2 — Use it in the owner functions, with back-compat fallback. Introduce a single private helper so the '[workspace] run N' phrasing has ONE home (do not duplicate the string in both functions). Behavior:
  - When displayNumber is null → return run.id (unchanged, both functions).
  - When displayNumber is a valid number AND workspaceName is a non-empty string (after trim) → `${trimmedWorkspaceName} run ${displayNumber}` (e.g. 'CoCoder run 98').
  - When displayNumber is valid but workspaceName is absent/empty/whitespace → `workspace run ${displayNumber}` (UNCHANGED from today — this preserves every existing caller).
  - `coCoderRunReference` keeps appending ` (technical id: ${run.id})` to the same base, so with a name it reads 'CoCoder run 98 (technical id: run_242)'.
`runDisplayNumber` is unchanged. Do not change any call sites in this atom — additive only.

CHANGE 3 — Pin all branches in packages/core/tests/display.test.ts (extend the existing file, keep its current 3 tests passing unchanged). Add tests asserting:
  - runDisplayName({ id: 'run_188', displayNumber: 98, workspaceName: 'CoCoder' }) === 'CoCoder run 98'
  - coCoderRunReference({ id: 'run_188', displayNumber: 98, workspaceName: 'CoCoder' }) === 'CoCoder run 98 (technical id: run_188)'
  - empty/whitespace workspaceName falls back to 'workspace run 98' for BOTH functions (e.g. workspaceName: '' and workspaceName: '   ')
  - null displayNumber with a workspaceName still returns run.id (name is ignored when there is no number)

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK:
- The existing 3 tests in display.test.ts stay green (the no-name path is unchanged).
- Run and paste exact output: `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core test` (full core suite must stay green — many tests across record/status/prompts/runner assert 'workspace run N'; they must NOT change, proving back-compat).
- Typecheck: `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core run typecheck` (paste exit status).
- Scope is display.ts + display.test.ts ONLY. Do NOT thread workspaceName into adapters, runner, daemon, or prompts in this atom; do NOT touch other call sites. The single-home rule: the '... run N' phrasing must exist once (one helper), not copied into both functions.

Report: the exact diff, the core test output (pass counts, confirm the pre-existing 'workspace run N' tests are untouched and green), and the typecheck exit status.

data.reason: Owner emits real workspace name via a single runDisplayBase helper (one home) with unchanged 'workspace run N' fallback; all branches pinned; core suite 627/627 green (existing call sites untouched, back-compat proven) and typecheck exit 0.

data.task:
Thread the real workspace name through the UI live-data adapter so founder-facing run labels render 'CoCoder run 98' instead of the literal 'workspace run 98'. This consumes the owner change already shipped in @cocoder/core display.ts (runDisplayName now accepts an optional workspaceName). Stay inside packages/ui. Files: packages/ui/src/renderer/adapter.ts, packages/ui/src/renderer/live.ts, packages/ui/src/renderer/App.tsx, and tests.

CHANGE 1 — adapter.ts. Add an OPTIONAL `workspaceName?: string | null` parameter to these functions and pass it into the core runDisplayName call:
  - `adaptRunSummary(r: RunSummary, priorityNames, workspaceName?)`: change line ~239 to `displayName: runDisplayName({ id: r.id, displayNumber, workspaceName })`.
  - `adaptRuns(runs, priorityNames, workspaceName?)`: forward workspaceName into each adaptRunSummary call.
  - `adaptRunDetail(detail, priorityNames, workspaceName?)`: forward workspaceName into its internal adaptRunSummary call.
Keep the parameter OPTIONAL and last so existing callers/tests that omit it still compile and still get the 'workspace run N' fallback (do NOT make it required).

CHANGE 2 — live.ts. Thread the name through:
  - `loadWsData(oz, wsId, workspaceName?)`: pass workspaceName into `adaptRuns(dRuns, names, workspaceName)`.
  - `loadRunDetail(oz, runId, names, workspaceName?)`: pass workspaceName into `adaptRunDetail(detail, names, workspaceName)`.
Keep both new params optional and last.

CHANGE 3 — App.tsx. At each call site, pass the active workspace's NAME (available via the `workspaces` array — look up by id, e.g. `workspaces.find((w) => w.id === wsId)?.name`; at the first-load site line ~183 the variable `first` already has `first.name`). Update:
  - line ~183 `loadWsData(oz, first.id)` → pass `first.name`.
  - line ~297 `loadWsData(oz, activeId)` → pass the activeId workspace's name.
  - line ~407 `loadWsData(oz, wsId)` → pass the wsId workspace's name.
  - line ~420 `loadRunDetail(oz, runId, namesRef.current[wsId] ?? {})` → pass the wsId workspace's name as the new 4th arg.
If a workspace lookup is momentarily undefined, pass undefined (the fallback handles it) — do not crash.

CHANGE 4 — Tests. (a) Extend packages/ui/tests/adapter.test.ts: add a case proving that when a workspaceName is passed, adaptRunSummary/adaptRuns produce displayName 'CoCoder run 98' for a run with displayNumber 98, AND that omitting workspaceName still yields 'workspace run 98' (back-compat). (b) Update the atom-0 test packages/ui/tests/dashboard-runs-tab.test.tsx ONLY if needed — it renders the Dashboard directly with a fixture run whose displayName is already 'workspace run 98', so it should remain valid and green; do not weaken its heading-primacy assertion.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK:
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/ui test` fully green including the new adapter case and the existing dashboard-runs-tab/dashboard-awaiting tests (paste pass counts).
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/ui run typecheck` exits 0 (paste status).
- Scope is packages/ui only. Do NOT touch core, daemon, runner, or persona prompts in this atom (daemon/agent surfaces are the next atom). Keep all new params optional+last so no unrelated caller breaks.

Report: the exact diff, the ui test output (pass counts), and the typecheck exit status.

data.reason: Workspace name threaded (optional+last) through adaptRunSummary/adaptRuns/adaptRunDetail -> loadWsData/loadRunDetail -> App.tsx call sites via a workspaceName(wsId) lookup; adapter test pins both 'CoCoder run 98' and the 'workspace run 98' fallback; ui suite 167/167 green and typecheck exit 0.

data.task:
Make the daemon's founder-facing Oz chat/awareness surfaces speak the REAL workspace name ('CoCoder run 98') instead of the literal 'workspace run 98' or the technical id. Consume the owner already shipped in @cocoder/core display.ts (runDisplayName/coCoderRunReference accept an optional workspaceName on their input). Stay inside packages/daemon. The thread: the daemon already resolves the workspace (it has workspace.name) when it computes displayNumber — attach the name on the same path so every runDisplayName(run) consumer renders the real name for free.

KEY LANDMINE — DO NOT TOUCH durable-record / commit-message paths. commitMessage (packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts) and the launcher post-wrap commit (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts ~1100) already PREPEND 'via CoCoder ' to coCoderRunReference. Threading a workspace name into THOSE run objects would double it ('CoCoder CoCoder run 98') and they are durable records anyway. This atom must NOT modify the runner, prompts.ts, agent-step.ts, launcher.ts, commit messages, or persona files — those are later/out of scope. Scope is the daemon Oz conversational surface only.

CHANGE 1 — packages/daemon/src/run-display.ts. Add `readonly workspaceName: string | null` to `RunWithDisplayNumber`. The functions already resolve the workspace (findWorkspace returns a RegistryWorkspace with `.name`; withPortableDisplayNumberForPath takes a path). Attach the name:
  - `withPortableDisplayNumberForPath(run, workspacePath, workspaceName?)`: add an optional workspaceName param (last) and set `workspaceName: workspaceName ?? null` on the returned object.
  - `withPortableDisplayNumber` and `withPortableDisplayNumbers`: they already call findWorkspace — pass `workspace.name` into the path helper (and set null in the no-workspace branch).

CHANGE 2 — packages/daemon/src/oz-awareness.ts. Add `readonly workspaceName: string | null` to `OzAwarenessRun`. Widen `OzAwarenessInput.runs` so it may carry workspaceName (e.g. accept `readonly (Run & { readonly displayNumber?: number | null; readonly workspaceName?: string | null })[]`, or a small local type) without breaking existing Run[] callers. In `projectRun`, set `workspaceName: (run as { workspaceName?: string | null }).workspaceName ?? null`. Because OzAwarenessRun now has id + displayNumber + workspaceName, the existing `runDisplayName(run)` calls in oz-chat.ts (runSummary, runListSummary), oz-host.ts (formatRun), and oz-context-pointer.ts (slugLabel) will emit 'CoCoder run 98' with NO change at those call sites — leave those call sites as-is.

CHANGE 3 — packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts buildPrompt (~line 196). Pass `target.workspace.name` as the new 3rd arg to `withPortableDisplayNumberForPath(run, target.workspace.path, target.workspace.name)` so the awareness digest the Oz agent reads uses the real name.

CHANGE 4 — packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts launchReply (~line 385). Make the 'Launched {priorityId} as {runLabel}' reply use the real workspace name: thread the workspace name into launchReply (the callers at ~171/180 have `workspaceId` and `ctx` — resolve the name from the daemon registry/context that is already available, e.g. findWorkspace, or whatever workspace object the handler holds) and pass it as workspaceName into `runDisplayName({ id: runId, displayNumber, workspaceName })`. If the name cannot be resolved, fall back gracefully (omit it; do not crash) — the owner's fallback yields 'workspace run N'.

CHANGE 5 — Tests. Update packages/daemon/tests/oz-awareness.test.ts: the existing `toEqual` assertions must include the new `workspaceName` field (it will be null for those fixtures since the runs carry no name) — this is adding the field, NOT weakening. Then ADD a focused assertion proving the real-name path: build an OzAwarenessRun input whose run carries workspaceName 'CoCoder' and displayNumber 98 and assert that the founder-facing summary output (e.g. via the exported runSummary/runListSummary if accessible, or by asserting runDisplayName picks it up through the projected run) renders 'CoCoder run 98'. If runSummary/runListSummary are not exported, add a small daemon test that constructs the projected run and asserts the rendered label. Also add/extend a test for run-display.ts attaching workspaceName if a suitable harness exists; otherwise the oz-awareness assertion is sufficient.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK:
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/daemon test` fully green incl. the updated/added oz-awareness assertions (paste pass counts).
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/daemon run typecheck` exits 0 (paste status).
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core test` STILL green — you must not have touched core; run it to prove commit-message/durable-record behavior is unchanged (no 'CoCoder CoCoder' doubling anywhere).
- Confirm by grep that you did NOT edit runner.ts, prompts.ts, agent-step.ts, launcher.ts, or any packages/personas/** file.

Report: the exact diff, the daemon test output (pass counts), the core test result (proving untouched/green), and the typecheck exit status.

data.reason: workspaceName threaded through run-display -> OzAwarenessRun so oz-chat/oz-host/context-pointer render 'CoCoder run 98' with no call-site churn; status & launch chat tests now pin the real name end-to-end; landmine respected (singular stays name-less for launcher's durable 'via CoCoder' commit, no doubling); daemon 383/383, core 627/627 green, typecheck 0; no forbidden paths touched.

data.task:
Complete the 'make the [workspace] slot real' goal on the runner's two remaining FOUNDER-FACING surfaces so they say 'CoCoder run 98' instead of literal 'workspace run 98': the Deb run-status feed and the WRAP-UP READY delivery. Consume the owner already shipped in @cocoder/core display.ts (runDisplayName accepts an optional workspaceName). Files: packages/core/src/runner/status.ts, packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, and tests. (prompts.ts buildWrapupDelivery already calls runDisplayName(run) — it needs no edit if the caller passes a name-carrying run.)

HARD LANDMINE — DO NOT double the 'CoCoder ' prefix and DO NOT touch durable records. In runner.ts the shared `runDisplay = { id, displayNumber }` (name-LESS) feeds DURABLE commit messages and labels that ALREADY prepend 'CoCoder': `runReference` (lines ~514, used at ~1104 'oscar-support ... via CoCoder run' and ~1155 'run-history ... via CoCoder'), `commitMessage(priority.id, runDisplay, n)` (~1351), and `formatGroupLabel` (~528). You MUST leave the shared `runDisplay` name-less and leave all of those untouched. Only the two founder-facing renders below get a name-carrying variant. Adding a name to the commit paths would produce 'CoCoder CoCoder run 98' — forbidden.

CHANGE 1 — Deb status feed (status.ts). The input field is `runDisplay?: Pick<RunDisplayInput, 'displayNumber'> | null` (~line 188) and line ~200 computes `displayName = runDisplayName({ id: runId, displayNumber: input.runDisplay?.displayNumber ?? null })`. Widen the Pick to also include 'workspaceName' (`Pick<RunDisplayInput, 'displayNumber' | 'workspaceName'>`) and pass it through: `runDisplayName({ id: runId, displayNumber: input.runDisplay?.displayNumber ?? null, workspaceName: input.runDisplay?.workspaceName ?? null })`. The render shape stays '# Run status — {displayName}' with the separate '- **Technical id:** `{runId}`' line — no prefix, no doubling.

CHANGE 2 — runner.ts call site for status (~line 721). Pass the workspace name into renderDebStatus WITHOUT mutating the shared runDisplay: change the call to `renderDebStatus({ ..., runDisplay: { ...runDisplay, workspaceName: workspace.name || null }, ... })`. `workspace` (the resolved workspace, already used for workspace.name at ~528) is in scope here.

CHANGE 3 — runner.ts WRAP-UP delivery (~line 1540) and wrap-play task (~line 1324). These pass/call the shared `runDisplay` to founder-facing text:
  - ~1540: `buildWrapupDelivery(runDisplay, pickup, outcome)` → pass a name-carrying variant: `buildWrapupDelivery({ ...runDisplay, workspaceName: workspace.name || null }, pickup, outcome)`. buildWrapupDelivery renders 'WRAP-UP READY for {runDisplayName(run)}' (no prefix) and still uses run.id for the commit-support command — both fine.
  - ~1324: the wrap-play task string starts with `${runDisplayName(runDisplay)} on priority ...` → change to `runDisplayName({ ...runDisplay, workspaceName: workspace.name || null })` so the wrap dispatch task also reads 'CoCoder run 98'.
Define a single local `const runDisplayNamed = { ...runDisplay, workspaceName: workspace.name || null }` near where runDisplay is created (~513) and reuse it at the three founder-facing sites (changes 2 short-form, 3a, 3b) for elegance — but DO NOT substitute it at the commit/label sites (1104/1155/1351/528). Keep `runDisplay` (name-less) exactly as-is at those.

CHANGE 4 — Tests. (a) status.test.ts: add/extend an assertion that when runDisplay carries workspaceName 'CoCoder' and displayNumber 98, the rendered markdown heading is '# Run status — CoCoder run 98' (and the '- **Technical id:**' line still shows the run_ id). Keep existing display-number assertions green. (b) prompts.test.ts / runner.test.ts: add an assertion that buildWrapupDelivery with a name-carrying run renders 'WRAP-UP READY for CoCoder run 98.'. (c) CRITICAL regression guard: confirm commitMessage still renders 'workspace run N' (NOT 'CoCoder run N') — keep/verify the existing commitMessage assertion in prompts.test.ts unchanged and green, proving the durable record was not touched and there is no doubling.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK:
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core test` fully green incl. new assertions (paste pass counts). The pre-existing commitMessage 'workspace run N' / '(technical id: run_XXX)' durable-record assertions MUST remain unchanged and green.
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core run typecheck` exits 0 (paste status).
- Grep-confirm you did NOT change the commit-message/label sites (runner.ts ~528, ~1104, ~1155, ~1351) or prompts.ts commitMessage, and that no output anywhere reads 'CoCoder CoCoder'.
- Scope: status.ts, runner.ts, and core tests only. Do not touch the daemon, ui, or persona files.

Report: the exact diff, the core test output (pass counts, confirm commitMessage durable assertion unchanged/green), and the typecheck exit status.

data.reason: A single runDisplayNamed variant feeds only the 3 founder-facing renders (Deb status heading, wrap task, wrap delivery) -> now 'CoCoder run 98'; shared name-less runDisplay untouched at runReference/commitMessage/label sites; commitMessage durable assertion still 'via CoCoder workspace run 1 (technical id: run_178)' (no doubling); status/wrap real-name pins added; core 629/629 green, typecheck 0, no out-of-scope files.

data.waitCondition: wrap-up prepared; final delivery pending landing outcome
